behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watch behavior (recursive directory event delivery + path handoff). MOCK-MISS: bug appears at watcher/OS boundary, not just local function logic. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE are both yes: mocked watcher events can falsely pass even if real recursive watching is misconfigured. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only filesystem daemon behavior, with no AI/LLM step (disqualifies Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp root directory, create a nested subdirectory, write a file inside it, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: request intake + dedup state + dispatch suppression over time window. MOCK-MISS: this behavior is not only one pure function if dedup must block downstream processing calls. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess and no AI/LLM output dependency, so no upgrade to System/Agentic/Workflow. Rejected System due behavior text focusing on dedup request logic within configurable window, which can be exercised in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake service, dedup store, and processing dispatcher in-process with a fake clock; send two requests for identical path inside the configured window and assert dispatcher is invoked once, then advance clock past window and assert a new request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS duplicate filesystem events; failure appears with real watcher/event semantics. REAL-INFRA is yes and MOCK-HIDE is yes: synthetic single-event mocks can miss duplicate-event races. Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM decisions or multi-agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real filesystem watching, perform one file write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write + rename or repeated flush), and assert only one processing/output record is produced for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is live OS directory lifecycle events (delete/recreate) and watcher re-subscription behavior. REAL-INFRA yes: this is fundamentally OS/filesystem event behavior. MOCK-HIDE yes: mocked watch streams may not reproduce invalidated handles and reattach timing. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is purely daemon+filesystem resilience.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real temp watch path, delete the watched directory while running, recreate it, then create a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering correctness depends on interaction between real file arrival timing, filesystem timestamp metadata, and concurrent event intake. REAL-INFRA likely yes because timestamp granularity and event ordering are OS/filesystem-dependent; mocks can hide true ordering bugs. Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM dependence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously in the real watched directory with controlled creation times, run daemon, and assert processing order (or emitted sequence IDs) matches creation timestamp order, including deterministic tie-break behavior if timestamps collide.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real write-in-progress race: filesystem event can fire before writer finishes, and daemon must defer processing until stable/complete. REAL-INFRA yes and MOCK-HIDE yes: in-process fakes often do atomic writes and miss partial-write timing hazards. Rejected Agentic/Workflow since behavior text has no model-driven decision stage.
behaviors.test_description: Using real filesystem writes, open a file and write it in chunks with delays while daemon is running; assert processing does not start mid-write and only occurs after final chunk/close, with output matching full file contents.
